title: HUD Announces Updated REO Disposition Guidelines for 2026
description: The Department of Housing and Urban Development released new guidelines for the disposition of Real Estate Owned properties, affecting how banks and servicers handle foreclosed assets.
content_preview: HUD has announced updated guidelines for the Real Estate Owned (REO) disposition process that will take effect in Q2 2026. The new rules aim to streamline the sale of government-owned foreclosed properties and increase opportunities for owner-occupant buyers.

title: Foreclosure Activity Rises 8% in February as Pandemic Moratoriums Fully Expire
description: New data shows foreclosure filings increased across the US in February 2026, with the highest activity in Florida, Illinois, and Ohio.
content_preview: According to ATTOM's latest U.S. Foreclosure Market Report, there were 35,196 U.S. properties with foreclosure filings in February 2026, up 8 percent from the previous month and up 12 percent from a year ago.

title: Wells Fargo Expands REO Agent Network in Southeast Markets
description: Wells Fargo is actively recruiting field service agents and REO listing agents across Florida, Georgia, and the Carolinas to handle increased foreclosure volume.
content_preview: Wells Fargo has begun expanding its network of REO listing agents and field service providers in the Southeast, signaling expectations of continued growth in bank-owned property inventory through 2026.

title: Commercial REO Volume Expected to Peak in Q3 2026, Analysts Say
description: Industry analysts predict commercial real estate foreclosures will reach their highest point in the third quarter, driven by office and retail property distress.
content_preview: Multiple industry forecasters now project that commercial REO volume will peak in Q3 2026 as office and retail properties continue to face valuation pressures and loan maturities force lender decisions.

title: New Legislation Would Require 90-Day Owner-Occupant Exclusivity on All REO Sales
description: A bipartisan bill introduced in Congress would mandate a 90-day exclusive marketing period for owner-occupant buyers on all REO property sales.
content_preview: The proposed Homeowner First Act would require all financial institutions selling REO properties to provide a 90-day exclusive marketing window for owner-occupant and nonprofit buyers before opening sales to investors.
